feedback.text: I would like to leave additional feedback with respect to recent events that concerned online payment processors and the Steam online videogame store. Apparently, an online activist group from Australia pressured payment processor companies so that they would block payments going through Steam because of certain games they condemned based on their moral views regarding fiction. This led Steam to having to remove thousands of games from their online store. I find it very concerning that an activist group from literally the other side of the world can pressure payment companies into stopping me from buying games on an online store in a third continent; not because I would be interested in buying such games, but because I find it most concerning that online payment processors managed to have such an oligopoly over internet transactions that we must now also fear that they will enact corporate censorship. Ever since the French Revolution, freedom of speech has been a cornerstone of European values, and I would like to see creative freedom of expression protected from corporate censorship based on nebulous statements of "brand protection".
